tax: Ignis Elementalis Minor is placed at species rank beneath Ignis (Pyric order, Fire Elementals class). Diagnosis rests on a compact sub-1.8 m blue-white flame humanoid with a char-ring under 35 cm and hotter contact burns than the standard orange form. It is separated from its sibling Ignis elementalis by its compact sub-1.8 m blue-white body from a narrow high-pressure vent versus the standard form's 2–3 m orange-yellow body from a broad fuel gallery; the two share the genus body-plan but never the the broad-gallery orange standard form. Composition, not colour or menace, fixes the placement: hotter blue-white combustion in a compact humanoid shape sustained by a narrow high-pressure vent jet.
ap: Ignis Elementalis Minor stands 1.2–1.8 m, hotter but smaller than standard. Its silhouette is a compact blue-white flame humanoid with a brilliant near-ultraviolet core. Three diagnostic surface markers separate it in the field: blue-white combustion colour — the hottest visible fire colour — versus the orange-yellow of the standard form; char-rings under 35 cm (versus 50–60 cm standard), but charred to 5 cm depth (deeper than standard's 2 cm) due to higher temperature; and near-ultraviolet flickers at the core edge that may cause temporary afterimage effects in crews observing it directly. It moves with faster movement than the standard form, darting between vent-jets rather than striding, and shows blue-white, intensely bright, causing temporary afterimage at under 20 m in darkness. Even after dispersal it can be identified from small, deep char-rings and blue-white ash wall-marks with an afterimage-bright soot, so a survey team need not see the living body to log the species.
eco: Ignis Elementalis Minor persists in narrow high-pressure vent jets, crack-fuelled galleries, and tight combustion passages of the upper-mid tiers where pressure is concentrated into a narrow stream. It does not breed; it forms where a narrow high-pressure vent jet ignites a concentrated combustion form into a compact humanoid shape, and recurs whenever the jet re-pressurises and the compact blue-white form re-ignites from the same vent crack. It sustains its mass on narrow high-pressure vent jet fuel, drawing in concentrated fuel from the jet to sustain the hotter core. Range is bounded by the pressurised vent jet zone: outside that band the body loses the concentrated fuel and fails faster than the standard form, because it has no broad gallery fuel to fall back on. Juvenile or nascent forms appear as a vent crack where the gas-flame has a blue-white colour and is stationary against a significant draught, which lets a surveyor predict where an adult will rise.
beh: Behaviour is the behaviour of its medium given intent. Ignis Elementalis Minor is roused by approach to within 3 m of its vent crack or a change in the draught that affects its fuel stream and otherwise lies a still blue-white jet-flame at a crack, visually similar to a high-pressure gas leak. It claims a single vent jet and the passage immediately around it and answers intrusion with rapid darting ignition passes rather than the standard form's sustained advance — it hits and retreats to its vent rather than corraling. Toward grey-folk it is a hit-and-retreat hazard rather than a sustained one; survey crews report that Lanternkin are instructed not to wave a blue-white jet-flame out — a flame that hot won't be extinguished by air movement and the attempt provokes a dart-attack. It is not cultural and keeps no craft — its 'decisions' are thresholds of pressure, heat, current, or charge, not plans.
traitsText: Stable characters of Ignis Elementalis Minor: a body of hotter blue-white combustion in a compact humanoid shape sustained by a narrow high-pressure vent jet; immune to fire and heat; also harder to smother than standard because the narrow vent keeps repressurising through partial smothering; and a defining weakness — total fuel cut-off and water flooding, which the standard form also fails to. Its senses run through the medium itself: fuel-pressure and radiant-heat sensing through the vent crack; it tracks heat signatures entering its pressure zone. It tolerates darkness, high pressure, and narrow confinement, which it actually prefers but fails at total fuel cut-off and water flooding. Mass varies with available element, yet the proportions, the blue-white colour, sub-35 cm char-ring depth of 5 cm, and dart-attack pattern, stay constant, which is why mass alone never reclassifies the taxon.
abilities: Abilities of Ignis Elementalis Minor are bounded by its anatomy, not borrowed from other elements: it makes rapid darting ignition passes and returns to its vent; it ignites at higher temperature — contact burns are deeper than standard form; it feeds from a pressurised vent that partially resists smothering; and it can contract to a near-invisible blue spark at the vent crack, then re-expand. Each is a direct expression of narrow high-pressure vent fuel in a compact blue-white humanoid combustion form, so it cannot, for instance, sustain itself away from its vent jet, match the mass or sustained-advance of the standard form, or corral prey in a wide passage. The powers scale with body mass and fade as the body disperses.
bg: In the Throat, Ignis Elementalis Minor matters because its similarity to a high-pressure gas leak means crews have tried to extinguish it as a vent hazard and received a dart-attack; the blue colour is now a formal 'do not ventilate' signal in Lanternkin protocols. Grey-folk survey practice treats it as a narrow high-pressure vent hazard requiring fuel cut-off rather than ventilation, and its presence reshapes routes through the upper-mid vent-crack passages, now blue-colour-checked before ventilation decisions. Lanternkin notation: a blue-white vent flame that does not waver in a 3 m/s draught is a living Ignis minor, not a gas leak.
g: Ignis Elementalis Minor (Ignis elementalis minor) is the small-form fire elemental: a compact, hotter-burning Ignis under 1.8 m with a blue-white combustion colour, formed from a narrow high-pressure vent jet rather than a broad fuel gallery. Documented from the narrow high-pressure vent jets and crack-fuelled galleries of the upper-mid tiers, it expresses a single inner element as its whole anatomy — a compact humanoid body of blue-white flame with a dense brilliant core, hotter than the standard but smaller — rather than as flesh laid over an animal skeleton. Ignis minor burns hotter than the standard form because its narrow high-pressure vent concentrates fuel without the volume to sustain a larger body — it is a different formation condition, not a younger version. Field teams record it as a true elemental, not a beast wearing mineral armour: when it disperses it leaves a very small char-ring under 35 cm across and blue-white ash marks where the arms contacted the walls and no carcass.
